questions.q: Which numbered President of India was Neelam Sanjiva Reddy?
questions.explanation: Neelam Sanjiva Reddy served as the sixth President of India.

questions.q: In which year did Neelam Sanjiva Reddy begin his term as President of India?
questions.explanation: He assumed the office of President in 1977.

questions.q: In which year did Neelam Sanjiva Reddy complete his term as President of India?
questions.explanation: His presidential term concluded in 1982.

questions.q: Of which state was Neelam Sanjiva Reddy earlier Chief Minister?
questions.explanation: He served as Chief Minister of Andhra Pradesh before his national offices.

questions.q: Which high parliamentary office did Neelam Sanjiva Reddy hold before becoming President?
questions.explanation: He served as Speaker of the Lok Sabha earlier in his career.

questions.q: Neelam Sanjiva Reddy holds the distinction of being which kind of person to assume the office of President?
questions.explanation: He was the youngest person to assume the office of President of India.

questions.q: As Speaker of the Lok Sabha, which House did Neelam Sanjiva Reddy preside over?
questions.explanation: The Speaker presides over the Lok Sabha, the House of the People.

questions.q: The President of India whom Neelam Sanjiva Reddy became is the constitutional head of what?
questions.explanation: The President is the constitutional head of state of India.

questions.q: Neelam Sanjiva Reddy's career path notably moved from which office to the presidency?
questions.explanation: His path from Speaker of the Lok Sabha to President is reflected in his card subtitle.

questions.q: Roughly how many years did Neelam Sanjiva Reddy serve as President of India?
questions.explanation: He served from 1977 to 1982, a term of about five years.

questions.q: The President of India, the office Reddy held, is elected by an electoral college that includes which of these?
options: Members of Parliament and state legislative assemblies
questions.explanation: The President is elected by an electoral college of elected members of Parliament and state legislative assemblies.

questions.q: As President, Neelam Sanjiva Reddy was also the supreme commander of which institution?
questions.explanation: The President of India is the supreme commander of the armed forces.

questions.q: Which two of Reddy's earlier roles were at different levels of government?
questions.explanation: He was Chief Minister of Andhra Pradesh at the state level and Speaker of the Lok Sabha at the Union level.

questions.q: The normal term of office of the President of India, which Reddy held, is how many years?
questions.explanation: The President of India is elected for a term of five years.

questions.q: Reddy's presidency followed that of which immediate predecessor in office?
questions.explanation: Fakhruddin Ali Ahmed, the fifth President, preceded Reddy, who was the sixth President.

questions.q: The Speaker's office that Reddy once held is the presiding officer of which body?
questions.explanation: The Speaker is the presiding officer of the Lok Sabha.

questions.q: Andhra Pradesh, the state Reddy led as Chief Minister, is located in which broad region of India?
questions.explanation: Andhra Pradesh is a state in southern India.

questions.q: The President whom Reddy became aids the working of which legislative body as a constituent part of it?
questions.explanation: The Parliament of India consists of the President and the two Houses.

questions.q: Which sequence correctly orders Reddy's career as reflected in his card?
options: President, then Speaker, then Chief Minister
Speaker, then Chief Minister, then President
Chief Minister, then Speaker, then President
President, then Chief Minister, then Speaker
questions.explanation: He served as Chief Minister and Speaker before becoming President.

questions.q: Reddy's term as President fell in which decade range?
questions.explanation: His 1977 to 1982 term spanned the late 1970s into the early 1980s.

questions.q: Reddy's record as the youngest person to assume the presidency relates to which attribute?
questions.explanation: The distinction concerns his age when he assumed the office.

questions.q: The office of Chief Minister that Reddy held is the head of government at which level?
questions.explanation: The Chief Minister is the head of government of a state.

questions.q: Under the Constitution, the President's office that Reddy held is described in which Part dealing with the Union executive?
questions.explanation: The Union executive, including the President, is dealt with in Part V of the Constitution.

questions.q: The presidential election in which Reddy was chosen for his term is best characterised as which kind of contest?
options: A vote of the Rajya Sabha alone
A nomination by the Prime Minister
questions.explanation: Reddy was elected President unopposed, an uncontested election to the office.

questions.q: Which constitutional Article vests the executive power of the Union in the President, the office Reddy occupied?
questions.explanation: Article 53 vests the executive power of the Union in the President.

questions.q: The Speaker's office held by Reddy is provided for in the Constitution under which Article concerning the Lok Sabha's presiding officers?
questions.explanation: Article 93 provides for the Speaker and Deputy Speaker of the Lok Sabha.

questions.q: Reddy first contested the presidency in an earlier election before his successful term; in that earlier 1969 contest the elected President was which person?
questions.explanation: In the 1969 presidential election V.V. Giri was elected, contesting against the field that included Reddy.

questions.q: As Speaker, an office Reddy held, the presiding officer in the Lok Sabha may cast a vote in which specific situation?
options: Only in the case of a tie (a casting vote)
questions.explanation: The Speaker has a casting vote exercised only to resolve a tie.

questions.q: Which statement about the chronology of Reddy's two highest offices is accurate?
options: He was Speaker of the Lok Sabha before becoming President
He was President before becoming Speaker
questions.explanation: He served as Speaker of the Lok Sabha prior to assuming the presidency.

questions.q: The President's term of office, as held by Reddy, is counted from which event under the Constitution?
options: The date of the election result
The date the President enters upon the office
The first day of the calendar year
The dissolution of the Lok Sabha
questions.explanation: The five-year presidential term runs from the date the President enters upon the office.
